title: Web storage OPFS save import export and quota gate
summary: Validate Web storage, OPFS, save/load, export/import and quota failure behavior for the M6 product gate.
acceptance: OPFS save, load, export, import and quota-failure recovery are tested
Windows/Web save container interoperability is proven or recorded as a product-gate blocker
Error states produce user-facing and diagnostic structured reasons
No public save compatibility promise is made beyond the M6 gate

history.details: Instantiated from reviewed WM-0084 M6 DAG; keep proposed until dependencies are done.
Allowed tools/test-runner.mjs only to route the required pnpm test --filter persistence command to persistence tests; no other tools or test-runner behavior changes are approved.

Implemented Web OPFS save/load/export/import/quota gate evidence with structured UI diagnostics and Playwright coverage; recorded Windows/Web container interoperability as a blocker; added only the project-director-approved persistence filter route in tools/test-runner.mjs; required checks passed.

verified: No blocking findings. Verified OPFS A/B slot + manifest save path, SHA-256/size integrity checks, export/import/load/delete and quota failure recovery with old slot preserved; user-facing and structured diagnostics are present and limited to safe ids/sizes/checksums/reason codes. Windows/Web container interoperability is correctly recorded as blocked because Electron preload still exposes placeholder unavailable save ports. No sim-core/Worker authority, public save compatibility, schema/codec migration, dependency, baseline, or M5 artifact changes found. tools/test-runner.mjs only adds the approved persistence filter route. Required checks passed: typecheck, test --filter persistence, test:e2e --filter web-shell, taskctl validate, git diff --check, and pnpm quality.

Integrated verified Web OPFS storage gate: save/load/export/import/quota recovery pass, diagnostics are structured and safe, Windows interoperability remains an explicit blocker, and no public save compatibility or simulation-authority changes were introduced.

objective: Validate Web storage and save/import/export behavior required for product-gate decision evidence.
forbiddenPaths: public save compatibility promise beyond M6 gate
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: browser quota APIs are inconsistent.
Risk: save export may expose local paths or sensitive diagnostics.
Narrow scope exception: tools/test-runner.mjs may change only to route pnpm test --filter persistence to persistence tests; it must not alter other filters or test semantics.
File ownership is limited to persistence, platform and web storage UI surfaces.
ownerGate: Block on public save migration, codec change or compatibility commitment without ADR
Block on local path, secret or full-save leakage in diagnostics
Block if platform storage mutates authoritative simulation state
m0M5RegressionCoverage: Save/load changes must preserve M5 focused save/replay evidence or provide reviewed migration evidence
Simulation Worker remains the only world writer
benchmarkImpact: May add storage timing or quota evidence; does not update M0-M5 benchmark baselines.
reviewRouting: client-engineer completes to reviewer; reviewer verifies persistence/security boundaries.
rollbackModel: Revert Web storage gate changes and leave product decision blocked on storage evidence.

sparkEligibility.reason: Web persistence, save/export and quota behavior touch product and security boundaries.

review.summary: No blocking findings. Verified OPFS A/B slot + manifest save path, SHA-256/size integrity checks, export/import/load/delete and quota failure recovery with old slot preserved; user-facing and structured diagnostics are present and limited to safe ids/sizes/checksums/reason codes. Windows/Web container interoperability is correctly recorded as blocked because Electron preload still exposes placeholder unavailable save ports. No sim-core/Worker authority, public save compatibility, schema/codec migration, dependency, baseline, or M5 artifact changes found. tools/test-runner.mjs only adds the approved persistence filter route. Required checks passed: typecheck, test --filter persistence, test:e2e --filter web-shell, taskctl validate, git diff --check, and pnpm quality.

integration.summary: Integrated verified Web OPFS storage gate: save/load/export/import/quota recovery pass, diagnostics are structured and safe, Windows interoperability remains an explicit blocker, and no public save compatibility or simulation-authority changes were introduced.
